---
title: "Connectors are agents"
description: "A connector is not a static adapter but an agent that wraps a source, declares a capability, and files results with provenance; a small ontology per source makes it reliable."
date: "2026-07-02"
categories: [ecosystem]
---

Off-the-shelf sources (papers, PDFs, notes, tables) come into the wiki through the standard ingest path. Databases, APIs, and complex figures need a connector, and a connector is not a static adapter.

Extraction is interpretive work: reading a table out of a paper, reconciling a supplementary spreadsheet against the paper's notation, normalizing to a schema, attaching provenance and a confidence, retrying when a source is malformed. That is open-ended, so a connector is an **agent** that wraps a source, declares a capability, does the messy extraction, and files the result with its provenance. The researcher's hub agent discovers and orchestrates a fleet of them.

The reliability mechanism is a small ontology per source (see [Ontologies, kept small](../ontologies/)): reasoning over an explicit small ontology is accurate, guessing at raw responses is not. So a connector is finished not when it can call the API but when the source is described well enough to reason over without guessing.

We built one against Notre Dame's PAD service: it reads the service's own ontology and its OpenAPI spec (a machine-readable list of the API's calls), exposes one generic read-only tool, and answers live questions with no per-endpoint code and no interface at all ([see it working](https://la3d.github.io/WGRA/research-agents-revolution/pad-example/)). Discovery abstracts over delivery: the agent asks for a capability and does not care whether it resolves to a plugin it installs or a service it calls.

## Related

- [Ontologies, kept small](../ontologies/), the per-source description that makes a connector reliable.
- [A marketplace for tools](../marketplace/), the other way a capability reaches an agent.
- [Agent federation](../agent-federation/), where extracted knowledge gets shared.
